title: Predictive Processing Framework for Cognitive Prosthetics
abstract: Neural implementation of Bayesian brain hypothesis

body.text: This paper applies the predictive processing framework to cognitive prosthetic design, establishing that the brain operates as a prediction machine that minimizes prediction error through action and perception.
The predictive processing theory provides a unified framework for understanding perception, action, and cognition as hierarchical Bayesian inference.
Cognitive prosthetics should support prediction error minimization
Assistive technology must respect hierarchical processing levels
Interventions should target precision weighting of prediction errors
Modulate sensory input precision based on context and goals
This framework informs the design of all P31 Labs artifacts, ensuring they work with rather than against the brain's natural predictive processes.
